resources.string: This keyboard holds no internet permission — verifiable in the system. Nothing is "phoned home"; everything stays on the device.
Free software: GPL-3.0-or-later. Copyright © 2026 Froehlich Media.

AdaptKey records the exact raw touch position behind every letter you type, not just which key it resolved to, and continuously adjusts each key\'s own hit zone to your personal tap pattern - seeded from the typing style you chose during setup, then refined by every further keystroke.
Learns your personal tap pattern for every key from everyday typing — an optional short calibration gives it an immediate head start, and it keeps refining itself afterwards.
Missing diacritics (like German umlauts and ß) are restored automatically when unambiguous, or offered as the top suggestion otherwise — e.g. "konnen" → "können".
Fixes typos landing on an adjacent key and repairs a missed or an accidentally inserted space between two words.
A single backspace tap right after an autocorrection restores exactly what you typed, instead of just deleting a letter.
Long-press any key that has more than one alternative to choose from all of them at once, Gboard-style.
Common symbols (@, €, #, π, ×, ÷ and more) sit as long-press hints directly on letter keys, so you rarely need to leave the letter page.
Digits stay on a row of their own above the letters, each with its shifted symbol one long-press away; switch it off in settings if you prefer.
A left/right swipe anywhere on the keys cycles through the letter page and both symbol pages — no need to reach for the small "?123" key.
Swipe left or right on the space bar to switch which language you\'re typing in.
Swipe to delete a whole word
Swipe left on the backspace key to delete the entire previous word in one motion.
Swipe downward anywhere on the keyboard to hide it.
Drag a suggestion to the trash
Drag a suggestion up into the trash area that appears to permanently exclude it — a deliberate motion, so it never happens by accident.
Press Shift right after finishing a word to flip the case of its first letter — or, if you keep typing, the next letter is capitalised instead (handy for camelCase).
Clipboard content appears where the suggestion bar normally is; tap it to paste. Passwords are masked, and the clipboard is cleared right after pasting.
The suggestion bar predicts a likely next word before you\'ve typed a single letter of it.
A small language model can be downloaded once and imported to sharpen suggestions further — it runs entirely on the device, so the offline guarantee never changes.
German, English and Greek each have their own dictionary; the active one is detected automatically from what you\'re actually writing.
This keyboard requests no internet permission at all — check it yourself in the system\'s app settings.

Recognises login fields and offers your own previously used usernames and email addresses as you type. Every saved value can be reviewed or removed from settings any time, or saving can be switched off entirely — passwords are never touched or recorded, no matter what.
An optional, opt-in setting offers email addresses from your address book alongside your saved ones — permission is asked for right there in settings, never behind your back.
Dedicated keyboard for URL and email fields

A web address or email field automatically gets its own keyboard row (https://, www., a dedicated @ or slash) — switchable back to the ordinary keyboard from the extra row if you just want to search or type normally.
Review what the keyboard has learned
See every word AdaptKey has taught itself from your own typing, kept completely separate from the built-in dictionary, and remove any of them individually.
Autofill suggestions from your password manager appear right in the suggestion bar when available.
Long compound words are recognised and corrected even without a hyphen, from their known component parts.
Suggests the missing space in run-together words
Two words typed without a space in between are offered back apart, as a normal suggestion-bar tap.
Swipe up for the extra row
An upward swipe on the keyboard reveals a row of extra actions — emoji panel, clearing the clipboard, and a live view of your personal touch-zone model.
Narrower reduces accidental spaces instead of commas (L-02).
Percentage surcharge, taken from the neighbouring keys (L-04).
Percentage surcharge for the shift key (D-16). Preset by calibration; adjustable.

Corrections, diacritic restoration, and missed-space fixes apply automatically while typing. Turn off to keep every candidate available only as a tap in the suggestion bar - words are never silently changed, but a missed or spurious space is not corrected either.
Pause without input after which the suggestion bar re-sorts (0–600 ms).
Highlights a recognised, complete word while typing (S-05).
Persistent digit row above the letters (L-06).
How long to hold a key before its alternatives pop up, in ms (D-32).
Show the combined ?123 key. With this off, the key disappears and its slot stays empty (D-59).
Extra gap under the digits, in dp, to reduce mis-taps into the top letter row (D-55).
Extra gap above the space/enter row, in dp, to reduce accidental Enter presses (D-55).
Secondary long-press symbols as a corner hint (L-05). Long-press still works either way.
Resets the per-key assignment to the default mapping.
Set or remove a secondary symbol per letter (L-05).
Tap a letter to choose or remove its long-press symbol.
Permanently exclude words from suggestions and autocorrect (A-04).
Review or remove words the keyboard has taught itself from your own typing - including ones that never show up as a suggestion.
How long a removed learned word is remembered before it can quietly be learned again (1–30 days).
Review saved login values, remove one at a time, or clear them all.

Recognised usernames and email addresses are saved for later suggestion. Never affects passwords - those are never recorded regardless of this setting. Already-saved values keep being suggested either way.

Offers email addresses from your address book alongside your saved credentials, once allowed. Your contacts are read live and never stored in this app. Turning this on asks for the Contacts permission once, right here.
Contacts permission not granted - suggestions from contacts stay off.
Guards against surprising field capitalisation (0–500 ms).
No sentence start after a comma line
After a line ending with a comma (e.g. a mail salutation), the next line is not capitalised.
The n-gram confidence below which the mini-LLM is consulted (C-06). The LLM backend is not wired in yet.
Download and import the optional on-device model (C-06).
When off, the mini-LLM never runs, even if a model is installed - useful to save battery/latency without deleting the model.
The tier-3 mini-LLM (C-06) is optional. The model (~273 MB) is not bundled in the app. Download it once and then import it — afterwards the app keeps working fully offline.
No model installed. Tier 3 is inactive; the keyboard works as usual.
No browser found. Please download and import the file manually.
This app holds no internet and no storage permission. The download runs through your browser; the app only reads the one file you pick.

AdaptKey learns how YOU type — and stays completely offline while doing so.

• Provably offline: no internet permission at all — you can check it in the system. Nothing is ever "phoned home", and there is no tracking.
• Reads your actual taps, not just your typing: it remembers exactly where each tap really lands, so it can recover a word even when a slip landed on the wrong key entirely — not just a neighbouring letter. A short calibration gets it right from the start.
• Smart correction: fixes typos on neighbouring keys and missing accents (e.g. "komplezz" → "komplett"), and repairs missed or accidental spaces.
• Learns gently over time: suggestions and autocorrect keep improving, but only from a steady pattern — a single mistake never throws a key off.
• Three languages: German, English and Greek, each with its own dictionary.
• Optional on-device mini-AI for even better suggestions — downloaded once, still no internet.
• Free software (GPLv3), no ads, no accounts.

Optionally, a small AI (mini-LLM) can improve the suggestions when the normal model is unsure — entirely on the device. The model (~273 MB) is not included: you download it once via the browser and import it. Afterwards everything stays offline. You can skip this now and add it any time later.
Finally, tell AdaptKey how you usually type. It sets up realistic touch zones for your fingers right away, then keeps refining them gently as you type. Optional and changeable any time later.
You can reach this introduction any time via Settings → Info & Privacy → "Show introduction again".
Shows the first-run introduction again the next time you open the keyboard.
The introduction will be shown the next time you open the keyboard.
Resets your learned touch zones back to the defaults for your current typing style - not the style itself, and not the dictionary.
Your learned touch zones will be reset to the defaults for your current typing style. Continue?
Every saved username and email address will be deleted. This cannot be undone. Continue?
Tell AdaptKey how you type, so key detection fits you from the start (K-01).
How do you usually type? This sets up realistic touch zones for your fingers right away; ordinary typing keeps refining them gently from there.
Touch calibration resets to sensible defaults for %1$s. Continue?
Would you like to tell AdaptKey how you type? It sets up realistic touch zones right away. You can do this any time in the settings.
No saved usernames or emails yet.
Plays a short click on every key press (default: off).
A short vibration on every key press (default: off).
The coloured circles show where your key taps land on average (the dot) and how much they scatter (the circle).
This is how the keyboard adapts to your typing.
Nothing learned yet. Type for a while or start the calibration.

Keeps a rolling 1-minute, in-memory log of recent typing events to help track down rare glitches. Off by default, and never written to disk or sent anywhere unless you explicitly share it below. Password fields are always excluded, regardless of this setting.
Shows the current log and lets you copy or share it.
No entries yet. Turn on recording in Settings, reproduce the issue, then come back here within 5 minutes.
